findings.description: flush() opens and tears down a brand-new SMTP (and likely TLS, on port 587) connection for every single message inside the loop instead of reusing one connection, turning a single batched send into N full connection setup/teardown round-trips (10-100x slower at scale).
flush() sends fully serially and blocks on each sendmail with no pipelining/batching/concurrency, capping throughput at 1/per-message-latency.
